title: toxicodendron burkeanum
intro: Toxicodendron burkeanum, also known as the Western poison oak, is a plant species in the Anacardiaceae family that is native to the Pacific coastal region of North America. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic allergenic compound that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Despite its toxic properties, Toxicodendron burkeanum has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds. It can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although caution is advised when handling the plant due to its potential for causing skin irritation.
